opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Parent: 'The school nurse said he has a heart murmur, and I'm terrified his heart is going to fail in his sleep.' Child: 'I feel completely fine.'
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A school screening flagged a heart murmur in an otherwise completely well child; the parents are extremely anxious and want reassurance.

symptom_narrative.onset: the murmur was noted today at a routine school medical check; the child has no symptoms
symptom_narrative.duration: no symptoms at all - an incidental finding today
symptom_narrative.character: the child feels completely well; parents describe a 'whooshing sound' the nurse reported
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the child has no complaints; the parents are severely distressed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: none - plays competitive soccer without breathlessness, chest pain or fainting; no cyanosis; no frequent chest infections
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: none; normal growth and development, no cyanosis in infancy

family_history_as_reported.narrative: no family history of sudden death, congenital heart disease or inherited heart conditions mentioned

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: happy, thriving child in a safe, nurturing home; plays competitive soccer

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: parents terrified of sudden cardiac death in sleep after the school notification; seeking definitive reassurance

communication_style.typical_message_style: anxious parent doing most of the talking; child interjecting that he feels fine

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: no concerns - normal growth and development
